title: Government Checks States on Health Insurance Rules for Abortion
summary: The Trump administration is looking into 13 states that have rules saying health insurance plans must cover abortion. These states include California, New York, and others. The government says this might break a law called the Weldon Amendment, which protects health care groups that do not want to cover abortion because of their beliefs. The administration sent letters to these states to learn more. Some state leaders, like the governor of New Jersey, disagree and say they will protect their rules. This is part of a big argument between political parties about abortion and health insurance.

background_read: This article talks about a government action related to health insurance and abortion. In the United States, different states have different rules about what health insurance must cover. Some states say insurance should pay for abortion, while others do not. The federal government has laws like the Weldon Amendment that protect groups who do not want to cover abortion. This is a sensitive topic because people have strong feelings about abortion rights and beliefs. The investigation is part of a bigger debate between Democrats and Republicans on this issue.
Article_Structure: WHO: The Trump administration and 13 states like California and New York
WHAT: Investigations into state rules requiring health insurance to cover abortion
WHERE: In the United States, focusing on specific states
WHY: Because the government thinks these rules might break the Weldon Amendment law

perspectives.description: The Trump administration believes states with abortion coverage rules may be breaking the law and is investigating to enforce the Weldon Amendment.
Some state leaders, like New Jersey's governor, think the investigation is unnecessary and will defend their rules to protect reproductive freedom.
